message:
fix(h2): validate HPACK header name/value characters per RFC 9113

Add character validation to the HPACK decoder to reject headers that
violate RFC 9113 Section 8.2.1:

- Header names: must be lowercase ASCII (a-z, 0-9, token chars).
  Rejects uppercase letters, spaces, tabs, NUL, CR, LF.
- Header values: must not contain NUL, CR, or LF.
- Pseudo-headers (starting with ':') are permitted in names.

Without this validation, a malicious H2 peer could inject CR/LF
sequences into header values. While binary framing prevents direct
exploitation at the H2 layer, if headers are forwarded to an HTTP/1.1
backend (common in reverse proxies), the CR/LF becomes a header line
terminator, enabling classic HTTP response splitting / request
smuggling attacks.

Validation is applied in decode_literal() after decoding the raw string
but before constructing the Header or inserting into the dynamic table.
Indexed headers from the static/dynamic table are already validated at
insertion time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
